MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jul-21-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendants Alsop Louie Management LLC, Alsop Louie Capital 2, L.P.. Alsop Louie Partners 2, LLC's Motion to Admit Counsel Pro Hac Vice, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE.

Counsel Jesse Bernstein declares only that in the past two years, he has not applied for pro hac vice admission "to any Superior Court of the State of California." Rule 9.40, however requires that he identifies "[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 9.30(d)(5).) The information required is not limited to matters in the Superior Court. Plaintiff may file a new application with a corrected declaration.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
